industries.name: Atomic Clocks & Frequency Standards
uses: Rubidium-87 vapor cell atomic clocks use the 6.834 GHz hyperfine transition of ⁸⁷Rb to maintain frequency with accuracy of ~1 part in 10¹¹ — less accurate than cesium but far smaller, cheaper, and lower-power

Rb clocks are used as secondary frequency standards in GPS satellite receivers, mobile phone base station timing (5G requires sub-microsecond synchronization), network timing servers, and scientific instruments
Military communications: rubidium oscillators in portable, battery-powered frequency references for tactical radio synchronization and field navigation systems
Rubidium frequency standards serve as holdover references — maintaining accurate time for minutes to hours during GPS signal outages in critical infrastructure
Each GPS Block IIR and Block IIF satellite carries rubidium atomic frequency standards alongside cesium clocks for backup timing redundancy

companies.name: Microsemi (Microchip Technology)
companies.use: World's largest rubidium atomic clock manufacturer. Produces miniaturized Rb oscillators for GPS, telecommunications, and defense applications. Its SA.45s chip-scale atomic clock (CSAC) is the world's smallest atomic clock.

companies.name: Spectratime (Orolia)
companies.use: Produces rubidium atomic frequency standards for GPS and GNSS satellites. Supplied Rb clocks to Galileo (European GPS) satellite constellation. Acquired by Orolia (now part of Safran Group).

companies.name: Frequency Electronics (FEI)
companies.use: Produces precision rubidium oscillators for space, defense, and telecommunications applications. Its space-qualified Rb clocks have flown on numerous military and commercial satellites.

industries.name: Quantum Research & BEC
uses: Rubidium-87 is the workhorse atom for Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC) research — the first BEC was achieved with ⁸⁷Rb in 1995 (Cornell, Wieman, Ketterle — 2001 Nobel Prize). Its accessible laser cooling wavelengths (780nm, 795nm) and convenient hyperfine structure make it ideal
Laser cooling and magneto-optical trapping of Rb atoms for optical lattice clocks, atom interferometry (inertial navigation), and quantum simulation of condensed matter systems
Rb atom chip traps and Rb BEC systems for quantum computing research — Rb neutral atom arrays are a leading platform for quantum gates and quantum simulation alongside trapped ions
Rb spectroscopy cells for laser frequency stabilization: saturated absorption spectroscopy of Rb D1 (795nm) and D2 (780nm) lines provides absolute frequency references for laser systems
Rubidium magnetometers (SERF and Mx types) for ultra-sensitive magnetic field measurement in MEG (magnetoencephalography) brain imaging and geophysical exploration

companies.name: ColdQuanta (Infleqtion)
companies.use: Quantum technology company using rubidium BEC and cold atom technology for quantum computing (neutral atom qubits), quantum sensing, and atomic clocks. Its Albert device is a rubidium-based chip-scale atomic clock.

companies.name: Atom Computing
companies.use: Quantum computing startup using rubidium-87 neutral atoms in optical tweezer arrays as qubits. Demonstrated 1000+ qubit quantum processor using Rb atoms — the largest neutral atom quantum computer announced as of 2023.

industries.name: Specialty Glass & Medical
uses: Rubidium carbonate in specialty optical glasses: improves refractive index, UV transmission, and chemical durability in camera lenses, fiber optic components, and night vision device optics

Hyperpolarized rubidium used to polarize xenon-129 gas for MRI lung imaging — Rb vapor optically pumped by laser then spin-exchanges with Xe to produce hyperpolarized ¹²⁹Xe for ventilation imaging of lungs

Rubidium-82 (⁸²Rb) as PET cardiac imaging agent: produced from strontium-82 generators, ⁸²Rb is a potassium analog rapidly taken up by myocardial cells — used for myocardial perfusion imaging without need for cyclotron

Rubidium formate drilling fluids: extremely dense clear brine (up to 2.3 g/cm³) for very high-pressure well completions — used where conventional ZnBr₂ brines are insufficient for formation pressure control

companies.name: Bracco Diagnostics
companies.use: Produces the CARDIOGEN-82 rubidium-82 generator for cardiac PET myocardial perfusion imaging. The generator contains strontium-82, which decays to rubidium-82 used as the cardiac tracer in nuclear cardiology.

companies.name: Siemens Healthineers
companies.use: Major medical imaging company whose PET/CT scanners are used with rubidium-82 cardiac tracers for myocardial perfusion imaging. Manufactures the scanners that detect the ⁸²Rb positron emission from the heart.
